title: Haageocereus multangularis
intro: Haageocereus multangularis, also known as the "Mammillaria multangularis," is a species of cactus native to Mexico, characterized by its multiple angles and short, fleshy stems. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Haageocereus multangularis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Haageocereus multangularis is often used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, with the most common herbal preparations being teas and infusions made by infusing the plant's stems in hot water.
taxonomy: Haageocereus multangularis is a species of cactus in the family Cactaceae. It belongs to the genus Haageocereus, which is a group of columnar cacti native to South America. The classification of Haageocereus multangularis is further supported by its inclusion in the subfamily Cactoideae, a large and diverse group within the Cactaceae family. The species is also classified as a type of columnar cactus, characterized by its tall, upright growth habit. This classification helps to identify and distinguish Haageocereus multangularis from other species within the same genus and family.
